title: Cannot run podman in the containers started via Docker Compatible APIs while podman in the containers started via podman CLIs works
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issueWhen we run rootless podman in a rootful container and are starting the container using the GoLang Docker compatible APIs, we are seeing 

`newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted `

it doesn't seem to happen with the container manually started with the podman clis.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

The container id f08d4f340de2 was started via Golang docker compatible APIs, e.g. containerCreate, containerAttach, containerStart, containerExec, etc.

Then use "podman exec" command to enter into the container, run "podman ps" after switching to another user "tester".

```
[root@cloudshell1stvnic-719531 ~]# podman ps       // <-- CONTAINER ID: f08d4f340de2
[root@cloudshell1stvnic-719531 ~]# podman exec -it f08d4f340de2 /bin/bash
[root@0fcdcc7dd51a dev]# groupadd podman
[root@f08d4f340de2 dev]# useradd --gid dev --groups podman --uid 1101 tester
[root@f08d4f340de2 dev]# su tester
[tester@f08d4f340de2 dev]$ podman ps
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 1555 0 1101 1 1 165536 65536`: newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1

```

==========================
On the other side, if we create another container via the same image by using the podman run command, running "podman ps" after switching to another user "tester" works.

```
[root@cloudshell1stvnic-719531 ~]# sudo su -
[root@cloudshell1stvnic-719531 ~]# whoami  --> root
[root@cloudshell1stvnic-719531 ~]# podman images   --> image id: 26aa06bf1547
[root@cloudshell1stvnic-719531 ~]# podman run --rm -u root -it --privileged 26aa06bf1547 bash
[root@0fcdcc7dd51a dev]# whoami    // <- root
[root@0fcdcc7dd51a dev]# groupadd podman
[root@0fcdcc7dd51a dev]# useradd --gid oci --groups podman --uid 1101 tester
[root@0fcdcc7dd51a dev]# su tester
[tester@0fcdcc7dd51a dev]$ podman ps 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[tester@0fcdcc7dd51a dev]$ 
```



### Describe the results you received

Any podman command results in this error message:

```
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 1555 0 1101 1 1 165536 65536`: 
newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted 

Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1
```

### Describe the results you expected

Any podman command returns successfully:
```
tester@611b68fa1b1a dev]$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[tester@611b68fa1b1a dev]$
```

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
> The container id f08d4f340de2 was started via Golang docker compatible APIs, e.g. containerCreate, containerAttach, containerStart, containerExec, etc.

This is not helpful to us, you have to provide the exact options used to create the container.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
